name: Procedural Floor
description: A run-scoped floor generator that combines topology, placed rooms, encounters, rewards, shops, bosses, exits, difficulty pacing, and persistence for the current roguelike run.
design_notes: A roguelike procedural floor owns run pacing, not just room generation. It should combine run_seed, floor_index, content version, graph topology, room placement, encounter budgets, reward cadence, shops, bosses, exits, and meta-progression constraints into a floor that can be saved, resumed, replayed, and debugged. The generator should log seed streams, selected content, and validation failures so designers can reproduce bad layouts.
design_purpose: Create replayable layouts with authored pacing constraints.
Control the mix of combat, shops, events, treasure, exits, and recovery nodes per floor.
Prevent soft-locks by validating connectivity, keys, exits, and required traversal abilities.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are exploring an unknown floor that still seems intentionally paced and beatable.

parameters.name: run_seed
parameters.description: Run seed used to reproduce the generated floor.

parameters.name: floor_index
parameters.description: Run position used to select difficulty, biome, and room pools.

parameters.name: generated_floor_id
parameters.description: Stable floor identity used for save/quit/resume, map state, and replay logs.

parameters.name: rng_stream_id
parameters.description: Separated random stream so one content change does not disturb every roll.

parameters.name: room_node_id
parameters.description: Graph node identifier used by validation and save data.

parameters.name: critical_path_node_count
parameters.description: Required route length from entrance to exit.

parameters.name: branch_probability
parameters.description: Weighted likelihood of optional graph branches during layout build.

parameters.name: floor_reward_budget_id
parameters.description: Reward and shop budget applied to rooms, branches, bosses, and exits on this floor.

parameters.name: validation_retries
parameters.description: Fallback attempts before using a known safe floor template.

edge_cases: A locked room appears before the key or traversal ability needed to reach its reward.
The only exit is generated behind an encounter that can despawn or become unreachable.
A shop, rest room, or boss node occupies a layout socket too small for required actors.
Content version changes make an old run save point to a removed room_template_id.
An optional branch creates a loop that bypasses intended floor_index pacing or boss requirements.
Save/quit/resume occurs after rewards are generated but before visited rooms, shops, or exit state are serialized.
Daily seeds or reroll effects need clear rules for whether the same run_seed can produce a repaired or replaced floor.
Meta-progression unlocks change available room, relic, shop, or boss pools while an old run is still resumable.
common_bugs: Layout RNG and reward RNG share a stream, so adding a decoration changes combat rewards.
Validation checks connectivity but not collision, navmesh, or camera framing inside room templates.
Room graph ids are regenerated on load, breaking map reveal and visited-room persistence.
Fallback generation uses the same invalid constraints and repeats the same failure.
Debug logs record the run_seed but not floor_index, content_version, or rng_stream_id cursor.
A shop or boss room is selected correctly by topology but missing from the reward budget, leaving an empty required node.
Save reload rerolls unopened rewards because generated_floor_id stores layout but not reward stream cursors.
balancing_notes: Longer critical paths increase commitment and resource attrition; optional branches should compensate with meaningful rewards or information.
Too many safe rooms, shops, or recovery nodes can flatten tension even when combat rooms are difficult.
Daily or seeded runs benefit from deterministic layouts, but live balance changes need content_version handling.
accessibility_notes: Provide map readability for generated layouts, including visited state, exits, and branch types.
Avoid requiring players to memorize long procedural routes without landmarks or map support.
Keep fallback layouts available for modes that reduce disorientation or maze complexity.

implementation_notes.unity: Build a floor graph from ScriptableObject room templates using separate RNG streams for layout, encounters, shops, bosses, and rewards. Validate doorway sockets, navmesh bake, required keys, and exit reachability before instantiating, then persist room_node_id and generated_floor_id mappings for save/load.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent room templates as PackedScenes with doorway metadata and graph tags. Generate the room graph from run_seed and floor_index, run connectivity and collision validation, and store node ids, reward picks, and shop state in the run save so map reveal survives reloads.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Data Assets or level instances for rooms and a server-authoritative floor generation subsystem for networked runs. Log run_seed, content_version, floor_index, generated_floor_id, selected room_template_id values, and reward budgets to make bad floors reproducible.
implementation_notes.web: Generate graph data first, then render Phaser tilemaps or Three.js room chunks from the validated graph. Keep sorted template ids and deterministic RNG streams so browser, server, save, and replay tools produce matching floors.

relationships.reason: Procedural floors depend on placed room templates, socket compatibility, and room-level path validation.
Floor layouts need graph nodes, doors, critical path checks, and branch validation.
Run-scoped floors need deterministic seed streams, content versioning, and debug logs for replay, daily seeds, and save/resume.
Floor generation creates reward rooms, combat rooms, and event nodes that can distribute powerups.

example_games.title: Enter the Gungeon
example_games.note: Uses generated floor layouts with rooms, shops, bosses, and rewards.

example_games.title: Dead Cells
example_games.note: Uses generated level layouts inside biome and progression constraints.
